commit.message:
fix: commit dist/ to avoid build-during-install issues

npm's git dep preparation runs lifecycle scripts via cmd.exe in a
nested context where node_modules/.bin isn't reliably on PATH.
This caused tsc/npx to fail during prepare.

Ship pre-compiled dist/ instead — zero lifecycle scripts needed.

commit.message:
sessions: fix "Generate New Task" no-op on existing sessions (#308883)

* Fix 'Generate New Task' no-op on existing sessions

sendAndCreateChat only works for new (untitled) sessions or when
multi-chat is enabled. For existing/completed sessions it threw an
error that was silently swallowed, making the action appear to do
nothing.

Add a _generateNewTask helper that checks the session status:
- Untitled sessions: use sendAndCreateChat (existing first-chat flow)
- Existing sessions: send /generate-run-commands directly to the
  active chat widget via acceptInput

Fixes #308805

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review: use passed-in session, add sendAndCreateChat fallback

- Use session.mainChat.resource instead of re-reading activeSession
  from global state, avoiding stale-reference bugs.
- Fall back to sendAndCreateChat when no chat widget is found for
  the session, so the action is never a silent no-op.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Simplify _generateNewTask: consolidate sendAndCreateChat fallback

Both the untitled and widget-not-found paths called sendAndCreateChat,
so collapse the if/else into a single widget-first-then-fallback flow.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: signal-based engagement classification, simplified session recap

Replace length-based heuristic with engagement signal detection:
- Questions (?), reasoning connectors (but, because, however),
  opinions (I think, let's, we should), context-providing
  (the problem is, the issue is), corrections (no, actually, wait),
  and constraints (must, need to, make sure)
- Commands (/fleet, /chronicle) classified as interaction, not engaged
- System-prefixed messages (e.g. emoji + warning + '. ') stripped
  before delegation matching

Simplified session recap architecture:
- Removed lib/session-recap.mjs and SQL-based session queries
- session_recap uses live counters (authoritative, includes RPC mode)
- session_recap_debug uses persisted turnLog with message previews
- Added turnLog to counter persistence for per-turn tracking

Removed MIN_ENGAGED_LENGTH from constants (no longer needed).
42 tests covering signal-based classification.

commit.message:
civic: full multi-tenancy namespace scoping

Every per-user table (objects, deadlines, exceptions, module_configs)
is now scoped by namespace = town_id UUID. Changes:

- civicStore.ts: remove seedIfEmpty() call; add namespace column
  migrations for objects/deadlines/exceptions; migrate module_configs
  from single module_id PK to composite (namespace, module_id) PK
- civicRoutes.ts: all queries scoped by actor.town_id:
  - GET/POST /objects: filter/insert by namespace
  - GET/PATCH /objects/:id: add namespace check
  - GET /exceptions + POST /:id/acknowledge: namespace scoped
  - GET /deadlines: namespace scoped
  - GET /watch/feed: namespace scoped via objects join
  - GET/PATCH /setup: use actor.town_id instead of 'singleton'
  - GET /org-manager/status: queries scoped to namespace
  - POST /org-manager/town,staff,bodies: insert namespace column
  - GET/POST /org-manager/configure: per-namespace module configs
  - POST /org-manager/complete,identity: use actor.town_id
  - provisionCivicActor: each user gets their own town_profile + namespace
  - getCivicActor: resolves town_id from actor's object namespace column

Each new user now gets a completely isolated workspace with no
cross-contamination from other users or seeded demo data.

fix: AWS credential fallback robust against AWS_PROFILE set to missing profile (#1104)

* fix: AWS credential fallback should not require default profile in config

When a specified AWS profile is not found, the fallback was using
WithSharedConfigProfile("default") which still requires a [default]
section in ~/.aws/config. EC2 users with no config file at all would
panic with "failed to get shared config profile, default".

Remove the explicit profile override in the fallback so the SDK uses
its default credential chain (env vars, shared credentials, EC2 IMDS)
without requiring any profile to exist in config files.

Fixes #1094

commit.message:
Fix wrist quaternion: use exact parent rotation, not approximation

The previous computeWristQuat used setFromUnitVectors((0,1,0), forearmDir)
to approximate the parent rotation. This gives the correct Y-axis mapping
but WRONG twist around the forearm axis, causing the hand segment to
point in the wrong direction (paddle behind wrist instead of in front).

Fix: compute the exact parent rotation analytically as
R_y(-phi1) * R_x(phi2+phi3-pi/2), matching the actual Three.js hierarchy
(shoulderYaw * shoulderPitch * elbow). Then transform the desired paddle
normal to wrist-local space and use setFromUnitVectors to get the wrist
quaternion.

FK verification: 0.0mm position error AND exact normal match for all
test positions and tilt values.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: allow PENDING purchases to proceed through report generation

generateReportForPurchase blocked PENDING status, causing purchases to
silently fail when no Stripe webhook arrived. PENDING is now treated the
same as PAID so admin-triggered (or free) purchases never get stuck.

commit.message:
Merge open PR fixes into main-ready branch

* security: fix insecure temporary file and directory creation

Addresses a security vulnerability where deterministic temporary file and lock
file names could be manipulated via symlink attacks in potentially shared
temporary directories.

This fix implements secure, per-task work directories in both the Bash and
Python network utility modules. These directories are created with restricted
permissions (700) and are explicitly verified to be real directories owned
by the current user before use, effectively mitigating symlink and race
condition attacks.

Changes:
- scripts/lib/network.sh: Implement per-task work_dir with ownership and
  symlink verification. Update cookie_file to be task-specific.
- scripts/utils/network.py: Implement _get_secure_work_dir with mode,
  ownership, and symlink verification. Update download functions to use it.
- tests/test_network.py: Update tests to match the new secure directory
  structure.
- Fix a PermissionError in Python tests by handling chmod failures in
  restricted environments.

Co-authored-by: google-labs-jules[bot] <161369871+google-labs-jules[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Ven0m0 <82972344+Ven0m0@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: optimize build_cli_args by hoisting dictionary lookups

Move redundant `.get()` calls for ArgMapping outside of loops in

* fix: handle int64 inverse/counts in unique_consecutive for nightly PyTorch

In PyTorch nightly, aten.unique_consecutive.default reports int64 for
inverse-indices and counts outputs in the FX graph even when input is
int32. The ONNX converter was using itype (input type = int32) for these
outputs, causing an AssertionError in _set_shape_and_type when it tried
to set the already-recorded int32 type to int64.

Fix: read per-output dtypes from sts['dtype'] (a tuple for multi-output
ops) and use inv_itype/cnt_itype for building inverse/counts ONNX ops.

Test update: test_aten_unique_consecutive_return_32 now casts expected
values to match ONNX output types, making it work for both old torch
(FX reports int32) and nightly (FX reports int64).

commit.message:
docs: personalized hypo-recovery and therapy diagnostics (EXP-1681–1688)

8 experiments on 1,827 hypo episodes across 11 AID patients:
- EXP-1681: Over-rescue dominates (53%), no minimal-rescue observed
- EXP-1682: Can't predict rebound at nadir (AUC=0.54)
- EXP-1683: Demand vacuum effect weak (r=-0.13)
- EXP-1684: Overnight hypos worst (69% → hyperglycemia)
- EXP-1685: Therapy settings don't predict hypo rate (n=11)
- EXP-1686: S×D equilibrium restored in 1.7h median
- EXP-1687: ALL patients show clustered hypos (26% cascade in 6h)
- EXP-1688: Hyper-rebound rate predicts TAR (r=0.791, p=0.004)

Key findings:
- 55% of cascading hypos follow hyper-rebound (hypo→hyper→hypo cycle)
- Post-hypo management drives overall glycemic control more than hypo
  frequency itself
- Patient k: 1.2 hypo/day, 0% hyper, 84.7% TIR = gold standard rescue

Renumbered from 1651 to 1681 to avoid collision with parallel research.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add mappers 135, 136, 137, 138, 139 (Sachen 8259 family + Sachen 3011) (#1930)

Implements five Sachen-board mappers:

- Mapper 135: Sachen 8259A (2 KiB CHR banks, shift=1, OR=[1,0,1])
- Mapper 136: Sachen 3011 / JV001 ASIC (6-bit latch/increment/invert chip)
- Mapper 137: Sachen 8259D (1 KiB CHR banks, reversed mirroring polarity)
- Mapper 138: Sachen 8259B (2 KiB CHR banks, no shift/OR)
- Mapper 139: Sachen 8259C (2 KiB CHR banks, shift=2, OR=[1,2,3])

Mappers 135/137/138/139 share a single Sachen8259 struct that selects
the variant from the mapper number at construction time. All four use
a mapper-internal 2 KiB CIRAM with per-quadrant nametable routing
controlled by register 7 (simple mode + 2-bit mirroring select).

Mapper 136 is a standalone struct implementing the JV001 ASIC copy-
protection logic (latch/increment/invert on a 6-bit register, output
applied to banks on 000 write).

Unit tests are included for all five mappers covering PRG/CHR banking,
nametable mirroring modes, reset, and snapshot/restore.

Closes #1029
Closes #1031
Closes #1033
Closes #1035
Closes #1037

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
engineering: Allow other ESP paths in validation (#586)

# 🔍 Description

Previously, the ESP (EFI System Partition) mount point was **hardcoded**
to `/boot/efi`. This PR makes the ESP mount path **configurable** via a
new `OverrideEspMount` enum on `FileSystem`, allowing Linux distros that
mount the ESP at `/boot`, `/efi`, or other paths to be serviced by
Trident.

## Behavior Changes

### Per-filesystem `is_esp` flag and `OverrideEspMount`
- New `is_esp: bool` field on `FileSystem` — replaces the removed
`FileSystem::is_esp()` method that compared mount points against a
hardcoded constant
- New `OverrideEspMount` enum (`UseDefault`, `Override`, `Block`)
serialized as `overrideEspMount` on each filesystem entry:
- **`UseDefault`** (default, omitted from JSON): `is_esp` is
auto-detected by comparing the mount point to `/boot/efi`
- **`Override`**: explicitly marks the filesystem as the ESP regardless
of mount path
- **`Block`**: explicitly marks the filesystem as *not* the ESP even if
mounted at `/boot/efi`
- Custom `TryFrom<FileSystemSerde> for FileSystem` / `From<FileSystem>
for FileSystemSerde` drives this logic during (de)serialization

### ESP validation in storage graph
- `check_filesystems()` now discovers the ESP mount path from the graph
and returns it as `Option<PathBuf>`
- New `StorageGraphBuildError::FilesystemEspMultiple` — error when two
filesystems are marked as ESP
- New `StorageGraphBuildError::FilesystemEspWithoutMountPoint` — error
when ESP filesystem has no mount point
- Removed `FilesystemIncompatibleSource` and
`FilesystemVerityIncompatible` error variants

### Host configuration validation
- `validate_inner()` obtains ESP mount path from
`StorageGraph::esp_mount_path()` instead of using a hardcoded constant
- New `HostConfigurationStaticValidationError::EspMountPointNotFound` —
no filesystem is marked as ESP
- `expected_partition_type()` and `check_partition_types()` receive the
ESP mount path as a parameter
- Removed `ImageApiMixed`, `InvalidSourceUrl`,
`UnsupportedSourceUrlScheme`, and `UsrVerityRequiresUkiSupport` error
variants (unrelated cleanup)

### COSI-derived host configuration
- `derive_host_configuration_inner()` now detects ESP partitions by GPT
partition type, sets `is_esp: true` on the first ESP filesystem found,
and warns if multiple ESP partitions with mount points are present

### `PartitionType` improvements
- New `PartitionType::as_dps()` and `From<PartitionType> for
DiscoverablePartitionType` — replaces the duplicated
`config_part_type_into_discoverable()` in `repart_mode.rs`

### Removed storage rule
- The ESP-specific storage rule in `docbuilder` (`filesystems.rs`) has
been removed since the ESP is now validated through the graph builder
(`check_filesystems`)

## Files with meaningful changes

| File | What changed |
|------|-------------|
| `crates/trident_api/src/config/host/storage/filesystem.rs` | Core of
the PR: `is_esp: bool` field, `OverrideEspMount` enum, `FileSystemSerde`
with custom `TryFrom`/`From` impls for (de)serialization logic |
| `crates/trident_api/src/config/host/storage/mod.rs` |
`esp_filesystem()` uses `is_esp` flag; `validate_inner()` gets ESP path
from graph; new tests for ESP with adopted partitions, non-default mount
paths (`/boot`, `/efi`), and missing ESP |
|
`crates/trident_api/src/config/host/storage/storage_graph/builder/filesystems.rs`
| `check_filesystems()` returns ESP mount path; validates no-mount-point
ESP and multiple-ESP errors |
|
`crates/trident_api/src/config/host/storage/storage_graph/builder/mod.rs`
| `StorageGraphBuilder` stores `esp_mount_path`; passes it to
`check_partition_types()` |
|
`crates/trident_api/src/config/host/storage/storage_graph/builder/partition.rs`
| `check_partition_types()` and `expected_partition_type()` call sites
receive `esp_mount_path` |
|
`crates/trident_api/src/config/host/storage/storage_graph/rules/mod.rs`
| `expected_partition_type()` signature takes `esp_mount_path` instead
of using the hardcoded constant |
| `crates/trident_api/src/config/host/storage/storage_graph/error.rs` |
New `FilesystemEspMultiple`, `FilesystemEspWithoutMountPoint`; removed
`FilesystemIncompatibleSource`, `FilesystemVerityIncompatible` |
|
`crates/trident_api/src/config/host/storage/storage_graph/validation_tests.rs`
| New tests for ESP validation; `is_esp` field added to existing test
fixtures |
| `crates/trident_api/src/config/host/error.rs` | New
`EspMountPointNotFound`; removed `ImageApiMixed`, `InvalidSourceUrl`,
`UnsupportedSourceUrlScheme`, `UsrVerityRequiresUkiSupport` |
| `crates/trident_api/src/config/host/storage/partitions.rs` |
`PartitionType::as_dps()` + `From<PartitionType> for
DiscoverablePartitionType` |
| `crates/trident/src/osimage/cosi/derived_hc.rs` | ESP detection during
COSI derivation via GPT partition type; warns on multiple ESP
partitions; new test for multi-ESP behavior |
| `crates/trident/src/engine/storage/partitioning/repart_mode.rs` |
Deleted `config_part_type_into_discoverable()`, replaced with
`partition.partition_type.as_dps()` |
| `crates/docbuilder/src/schema_renderer/node.rs` | `# INTERNAL` tag
stripping logic so internal-only doc comments are excluded from rendered
docs |
| `crates/docbuilder/src/host_config/storage_rules/filesystems.rs` |
Removed ESP-specific storage rule (now handled in graph validation) |
| `crates/trident_api/src/config/host/storage/filesystem_types.rs` |
Minor cleanup |
| `crates/trident_api/schemas/host-config-schema.json` | New
`overrideEspMount` property on `FileSystem`; `# INTERNAL` tags in schema
descriptions |
| `docs/Reference/Host-Configuration/API-Reference/OverrideEspMount.md`
| New doc page for `OverrideEspMount` enum |
| `docs/Reference/Host-Configuration/API-Reference/Storage.md` | Updated
for new field |
| `docs/Reference/Host-Configuration/Storage-Rules.md` | Removed ESP
storage rule entry |
| `report/esp-constant-usages.md` | Investigation tracking document for
ESP constant usage migration |

## Files with only minor `is_esp` field additions

These files only add `is_esp: true` or `is_esp: false` to `FileSystem`
struct literals in tests, samples, or runtime construction sites:

- `crates/trident_api/src/config/host/mod.rs`
